items.title: Expert slams WA as last state to close child safety loophole

children.content: Western Australia is the only state to not recognise Working with Children Check rejections from around the country, and has been "slow" to make other child safety law changes, expert says.

gallery.altText: Two people hand over a Working with Children Check card against a blue background.
A children's playground seen through a fence.
A child plays with puzzles at a table.

thumbnail.altText: Two people hand over a Working with Children Check card against a blue background.
A children's playground seen through a fence.
A child plays with puzzles at a table.

items.title: Firefighters who died battling blaze, from cancer added to honour roll

children.content: Hundreds of people have honoured the families and legacies of Western Australia's fallen firefighters at a memorial service in Kings Park on Sunday.

image.altText: A memorial service is held at the Firefighter's Memorial Grove in Kings Park.

items.title: Man, 18, dies after car rolls over on Perth's Mitchell Freeway

children.content: The man, who was the sole occupant of the vehicle, sustained critical injuries and died a short time later after being conveyed to Royal Perth Hospital.

image.altText: A long shot shows heavy traffic on the Mitchell Freeway.

items.title: Farmers say levees built to prevent flooding have made situation worse

children.content: In Western Australia's biggest food bowl region, farmers are still assessing the damage from the latest flood caused by Cyclone Narelle. However, farmers say the levees built to reduce damage made the situation worse.

image.altText: flooded plantations in carnarvon

items.title: Untold stories of women in whaling inspire art that delves into the past

children.content: The overlooked contributions of women in the whaling industry are the subject of an exhibition in the Great Southern region of Western Australia.

image.altText: eyes in a painting

items.title: Federal government to back 10 renewable energy projects in regional WA

children.content: WA's energy minister says the state will have the fuel it needs for this month, as further details are released about 10 renewable energy projects that are set to be built across regional WA.

gallery.altText: Amber-Jade sanderson wears a maroon blazer while sitting in her office, in front of a cabinet with photo frames and plants.
Minister Amber-Jade Sanderson gestures with her hands while speaking to a reporter
A hand holding a hose filling up a car fuel tank.

items.title: What's turning this red-tailed black cockatoo orange?

children.content: Spotted again in WA's South West, a rare condition is the reason for the bird's distinctive orange colouring.

image.altText: A distinctive orange and black cockatoo in a tree in Bunbury.

items.title: Work underway to bring plants to life after fire ravaged Stirling Range

children.content: Seed collections taken before major bushfires in WA's Stirling Range National Park give hope for the continued survival of flora species found nowhere else in the world.

image.altText: A woman standing on a mountaintop surrounded by plants

items.title: Ghost rainbows, storms and sunsets — WA's skies, captured by you

children.content: In classic shoulder season conditions, April delivered a transitional mix of warm beach weather and cool, wet days with rainfall in the double-digits as we inch closer to winter.

gallery.altText: Lightning crackles around the wing of a plane.
A dog sits on a beach
sun and clouds over a forest

thumbnail.altText: Lightning crackles around the wing of a plane.
A dog sits on a beach
sun and clouds over a forest

items.title: Dingo sightings near primary school put parents on edge

children.content: "Stressed-out" parents are wondering whether to let their children continue to get around unaccompanied, but wildlife advocates say the animals are not as threatening as their reputation suggests.

gallery.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

thumbnail.altText: A dingo glances towards the camera in the late afternoon light.

items.title: King Charles won over Trump with his visit but not Zohran Mamdani

children.content: King Charles's US state visit hit a bump in New York when Mayor Zohran Mamdani said if the pair were to speak, he would "probably encourage him to return the Koh-i-Noor diamond".

image.altText: King Charles standing next to Queen Camilla leans in to listen to Zohran Mamdani.

items.title: A year after the landslide, Albanese faces a harsher reality

children.content: The prime minister's second term is now defined less by what he wants to do than by what unforeseen events will allow.

image.altText: anthony albanese holds up medicare card

items.title: Australian taxpayers take on risk for major oil and gas projects

children.content: Australian governments take on risk to get major resource projects up and running, so why don't they take an equity stake?

image.altText: Close up of a collection of jagged red rocks. In the background, two towers spitting flames.

items.title: What a vote of confidence in NZ's prime minister could mean for the country's election

children.content: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon has survived a vote of confidence in his leadership amid a drop in approval ratings. Here's what this could mean for NZ's upcoming election.

image.altText: Two men shake hands while the other man looks happy but nervous.

items.title: Timika wanted to make a difference — and her bike project is doing just that

children.content: The Meekatharra community comes together to restore donated bikes, making a visible difference at the local school.

gallery.altText: Two kids each fixing a bike.
Composite: a man and a woman fixing a bike together; a kid smiles next to a bike
A large stone sign which says "Meekatharra" in front of red dirt.

items.title: Man who stabbed father to death with kitchen knife not guilty due to insanity

children.content: A Perth man who fatally stabbed his father has been found not guilty of murder on the grounds of insanity.

image.altText: A suburban street with multiple police vehicles and police tape.

items.title: Poliovirus detected in Perth wastewater

children.content: Poliovirus has been detected in a sample taken from a wastewater in Perth, the Health Department reveals.

image.altText: am illustration of a polio virus particle against a black background

items.title: 'Can't my last days be happy?' How James Valentine faced the end

children.content: Broadcaster James Valentine was offered two different treatments for his cancer, so he chose the one that let him keep doing what he loved. Australian Story followed Valentine over his final 12 months as he faced the end with courage, purpose and humour.

gallery.altText: Man in black T-shirt and shorts wearing thick-rimmed glasses seated on a large red couch. Walls covered in photo frames

thumbnail.altText: Man in black T-shirt and shorts wearing thick-rimmed glasses seated on a large red couch. Walls covered in photo frames

items.title: Aussies put Olympic heartbreak to bed with historic world title

children.content: Australia, despite not having a dedicated curling facility, has claimed a first ever world championship title, with Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt victorious in Switzerland overnight.

image.altText: Tahli Gill and Dean Hewitt hug

items.title: Miami GP to start three hours early as storm fears grow

children.content: F1's governing body announces the Miami Grand Prix will start three hours early, as fears grow about torrential rain.

image.altText: A man walks in the pits holding an umbrella.

items.title: Rare quadruplet birth stuns mum, 45, and doctors

children.content: A Kalgoorlie couple expecting triplets welcome a surprise fourth baby — delivering an extraordinarily rare set of quadruplets that stunned even their doctors.

items.title: Love story: When life changed in an instant, Lucy was Beau's rock

children.content: The goalposts moved dramatically on Beau and Lucy Vernon's life plans, but it brought them closer together.

image.altText: A woman in a white wedding dress standing next to a man using a mobility aid

items.title: They escaped scam compounds then saw their suffering 'gamified'

children.content: Scam centre survivors say they are hurt by games that serialise scam compound experiences online, but one creator says he hopes people find the game "fun".

image.altText: A man looks at the screen with the words Scam Center Simulator reflected in his glasses

items.title: Australian says Gaza aid boat activists were beaten by Israeli navy

children.content: Zack Schofield was one of three Australians taken to hospital after being released by Israeli authorities, and says he saw at least 10 of his crew being shot by rubber bullets.

image.altText: A young man in a light brown jumper speaks to a camera he's holding up in a kitchenette.

items.title: It's your Sunday serve of stumpers in our spelling quiz

children.content: You lot take your spelling seriously. Repeat after me: It's just an internet quiz. It's just an internet quiz.

image.altText: Guy Montgomery beams as he stands in front of the Guy Mont set in front of a podium bearing a cup, purse and bucket.

items.title: Nomadic family says 'dream is more powerful' than surging fuel prices

children.content: While many Australians are thinking twice about big road trips due to fuel costs, the Sharavnyam family from Mongolia is determined to fulfil a dream to travel the world.

items.title: 'Butcher of Bosnia' near death, say lawyers asking for early release

children.content: In a submission to a UN court, Ratko Mladić's lawyers say he has suffered a serious medical incident and should be released from The Hague on humanitarian grounds.

image.altText: A middle-aged man in a military uniform looks serious in a dated colour photograph.

items.title: 'Soundtrack to Australia': ARIA reveals six 2026 Hall of Fame inductees

children.content: For the first time in more than a decade, multiple artists are being inducted into the ARIA Hall of Fame.

image.altText: Two Tongan-Australian women pose in floral power suits, daisy yellow and powder blue respectively, on a marbled balcony

items.title: Hard-won legacy of Australia's longest strike in jeopardy

children.content: It's been 80 years since Aboriginal workers in the Pilbara up-ended a slave-like labour system. The descendants of those involved fear its legacy is being forgotten.

gallery.altText: The eyes of an elderly Aboriginal man with white hair.
An Aboriginal man with a grey goatee and wearing a beanie stares through prison bars.
The wayward door to a wrecked caravan, with broken windows.
